# K2M EdTech Awwwards-Level Landing Page Implementation Plan
## "From Confusion to Clarity" - Full Particle System Experience

**Date:** January 15, 2026
**Vision Level:** Awwwards Ambition (Option A)
**Target Timeline:** 32 Days (6.5 weeks)
**Generated by:** BMAD Party Mode Adversarial Review

---

## 🎯 Executive Summary

This document outlines a complete, battle-ready implementation plan for an award-winning landing page featuring:
- Pure black background with ocean mint (#20B2AA) accents
- GSAP ScrollTrigger particle coalescence system
- Lenis smooth scroll for luxury feel
- Cinematic "WHOA" moment with anticipation
- Mobile-optimized performance (45fps+)

**Core Philosophy:** "Disruption with emotional safety"

---

## 🎨 Visual Design System

### Color Palette
```css
:root {
  /* Backgrounds */
  --pure-black: #000000;
  --soft-black: #0A0A0A;
  --charcoal: #1A1A1A;

  /* Ocean Mint Accents */
  --ocean-mint-primary: #20B2AA;
  --ocean-mint-glow: #40E0D0;
  --ocean-mint-dim: #008B8B;

  /* Typography */
  --text-primary: #FFFFFF;
  --text-secondary: #B0B0B0;
  --text-muted: #6A6A6A;

  /* Borders */
  --border-subtle: #2A2A2A;
  --border-ocean-mint: rgba(32, 178, 170, 0.3);
}
```

### Typography
- **Headings:** Space Grotesk (modern, bold)
- **Body:** Inter (clean, readable)
- **Weights:** 400 (regular), 600 (semibold), 700 (bold)
- **Load:** Google Fonts with `font-display: swap`

### Visual Assets Needed
1. Territory Map SVG (inline, 200+ particle positions)
2. Particle system assets (2px circles, ocean mint gradient)
3. Gradient backgrounds (CSS-only)
4. Discord chat bubble icons
5. Emoji reaction graphics (heart, fire, bulb)

## 🏗️ Technical Architecture

### Core Stack
```javascript
Libraries:
- GSAP Core (v3.12+)
- ScrollTrigger Plugin
- Lenis Smooth Scroll
- Optional: Three.js (if 3D particles needed)

Build Tools:
- Vite (fast development)
- Tailwind CSS (utility classes)
- Autoprefixer (browser compatibility)

Performance:
- Lighthouse CI (CI/CD)
- WebPageTest (real-world testing)
- FPS monitoring (runtime)
```

### File Structure
```
/src
  /components
    /Hero
      Hero.js
      Hero.css
    /TerritoryMap
      MapParticles.js
      TerritoryMap.js
      Map.css
    /DiscordPreview
      ChatBubbles.js
      Discord.css
    /CTA
      CallToAction.js
      CTA.css
  /assets
    /particles
    /gradients
    territory-map.svg
  /styles
    token.css (color palette)
    typography.css
  /utils
    gsap-config.js
    lenis-config.js
    performance-optimizations.js
```

## 📋 Phase-by-Phase Implementation

### Phase 0: Foundation (Days 1-2)

**Objectives:**
- Initialize repository
- Install dependencies
- Set up build pipeline
- Create basic HTML skeleton

**Tasks:**
1. `npm init vite@latest k2m-landing -- --template vanilla`
2. Install dependencies:
   ```bash
   npm install gsap @studio-freight/lenis
   npm install -D tailwindcss autoprefixer postcss
   ```
3. Configure Vite, Tailwind, PostCSS
4. Create basic HTML structure with section placeholders
5. Set up Lighthouse CI in GitHub Actions

**Deliverables:**
- ✅ Repo initialized
- ✅ Dependencies installed
- ✅ Build pipeline working
- ✅ Lighthouse baseline score captured

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- `npm run dev` serves site at localhost:5173
- `npm run build` completes without errors
- Lighthouse Performance score > 80 (baseline)

---

### Phase 1: Visual Assets (Days 3-5)

**Objectives:**
- Create all visual assets
- Define design tokens
- Prepare Territory Map SVG

**Tasks:**

1. Create `/src/styles/token.css` with full color palette
2. Set up Tailwind config with custom colors
3. Import and configure fonts
4. Create typography scale (h1-h6, p, lead, small)

**Day 4: Territory Map & Particles**
1. Design Territory Map in Figma/Illustrator
2. Export as inline SVG with zone groups

**Day 5: Gradients & Supporting Assets**
1. Create gradient backgrounds (CSS-only)
2. Design Discord chat bubble graphics
3. Prepare emoji reaction assets
4. Test assets in browser for visual quality

**Deliverables:**
- ✅ Complete design token system
- ✅ Territory Map SVG ready
- ✅ Particle assets created
- ✅ All supporting graphics ready

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- All assets load without errors
- Visual quality matches design spec
- SVG is optimized (minified, no unnecessary markup)

### Phase 2: Core Animation Infrastructure (Days 6-9)

**Objectives:**
- Set up GSAP configuration
- Implement Lenis smooth scroll
- Create performance monitoring utilities

**Code Files to Create:**

**`/src/utils/gsap-config.js`:**
```javascript
import gsap from 'gsap';
import ScrollTrigger from 'gsap/ScrollTrigger';
import ScrollToPlugin from 'gsap/ScrollToPlugin';

gsap.registerPlugin(ScrollTrigger, ScrollToPlugin);

// Global defaults
gsap.defaults({
  ease: "power2.out",
  duration: 0.8
});

// Performance optimization
ScrollTrigger.config({
  ignoreMobileResize: true,
  autoRefreshEvents: "visibilitychange,DOMContentLoaded,load"
});

export { gsap, ScrollTrigger };
```

**`/src/utils/lenis-config.js`:**
```javascript
import Lenis from '@studio-freight/lenis';

const lenis = new Lenis({
  duration: 1.2,
  easing: (t) => Math.min(1, 1.001 - Math.pow(2, -10 * t)),
  direction: 'vertical',
  gestureDirection: 'vertical',
  smooth: true,
  mouseMultiplier: 1,
  smoothTouch: false,
  touchMultiplier: 2,
});

// Integration with GSAP ScrollTrigger
lenis.on('scroll', ScrollTrigger.update);
gsap.ticker.add((time) => {
  lenis.raf(time * 1000);
});

export { lenis };
```

**`/src/utils/performance-optimizations.js`:**
```javascript
// GPU acceleration helper
export function enableGPU(element) {
  element.style.willChange = 'transform, opacity';
}

export function disableGPU(element) {
  element.style.willChange = 'auto';
}

// Mobile detection
export function isMobile() {
  return window.matchMedia('(max-width: 768px)').matches;
}

// Image preload helper
export async function preloadImages(selector) {
  const images = document.querySelectorAll(selector);
  const promises = Array.from(images).map(img => {
    if (img.complete) return Promise.resolve();
    return new Promise(resolve => {
      img.addEventListener('load', resolve);
      img.addEventListener('error', resolve);
    });
  });
  return Promise.all(promises);
}

// FPS monitor
export function monitorPerformance() {
  let frames = 0;
  let lastTime = performance.now();

  function loop() {
    const now = performance.now();
    frames++;

    if (now >= lastTime + 1000) {
      const fps = Math.round((frames * 1000) / (now - lastTime));

      if (fps < 30) {
        console.warn('Performance drop detected:', fps);
      }

      frames = 0;
      lastTime = now;
    }

    requestAnimationFrame(loop);
  }

  loop();
}
```

**Deliverables:**
- ✅ GSAP configured and optimized
- ✅ Lenis smooth scroll working
- ✅ Performance utilities ready
- ✅ 60fps baseline established

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Smooth scroll feels luxurious (not jumpy)
- GSAP animations trigger correctly on scroll
- FPS monitor shows consistent 60fps desktop

**Objectives:**
- Build Hero component
- Implement text reveal animations
- Create gradient background shift
- Add ocean mint glow effects

**HTML Structure:**
```html
<section class="hero" id="hero">
  <div class="hero-background">
    <div class="gradient-layer"></div>
  </div>

  <div class="hero-content">
    <h1 class="hero-title">
      <span class="reveal-text">Using AI</span><br>
      <span class="reveal-text">but don't feel</span><br>
      <span class="reveal-text ocean-mint">in control?</span>
    </h1>

    <p class="hero-subtitle reveal-text">
      You're not alone.
    </p>

    <div class="hero-body">
      <p class="reveal-text">
        Most students we meet feel the same way.
        AI works sometimes. Confuses other times.
      </p>

      <p class="reveal-text">
        <span class="ocean-mint">Here's the truth nobody tells you:</span><br>
        The problem isn't you.
        It's that nobody taught you how to think with AI.
      </p>
    </div>

</section>
```

**CSS:**
```css
.hero {
  position: relative;
  min-height: 100vh;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  padding: 2rem;
  overflow: hidden;
}

.hero-background {
  position: absolute;
  inset: 0;
  z-index: 0;
}

.gradient-layer {
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  background: linear-gradient(180deg, #000000 0%, #0A0A0A 100%);
}

.hero-content {
  position: relative;
  z-index: 1;
  max-width: 800px;
  text-align: center;
}

.hero-title {
  font-size: clamp(2.5rem, 8vw, 5rem);
  font-weight: 700;
  line-height: 1.1;
  margin-bottom: 1.5rem;
  color: var(--text-primary);
}

.ocean-mint {
  color: var(--ocean-mint-primary);
}

.reveal-text {
  display: inline-block;
  opacity: 0; /* Animated by GSAP */
}

.hero-subtitle {
  font-size: clamp(1.5rem, 4vw, 2.5rem);
  margin-bottom: 2rem;
  color: var(--text-secondary);
}
```

**GSAP Animations:**
```javascript
import { gsap, ScrollTrigger } from '../utils/gsap-config.js';

export function initHeroAnimations() {
  const tl = gsap.timeline({
    scrollTrigger: {
      trigger: ".hero",
      start: "top top",
      end: "bottom top",
      scrub: 1
    }
  });

  // Gradient background shift
  tl.to(".gradient-layer", {
    background: "linear-gradient(180deg, #000000 0%, #0A0A0A 100%)",
    duration: 1
  });

  // Text reveals with stagger
  gsap.from(".hero-title .reveal-text", {
    y: 50,
    opacity: 0,
    stagger: 0.2,
    duration: 1,
    ease: "power3.out",
    scrollTrigger: {
      trigger: ".hero-title",
      start: "top 80%",
    }
  });

  // Ocean mint glow effect
  gsap.from(".ocean-mint", {
    textShadow: "0 0 50px rgba(32, 178, 170, 0)",
    duration: 1.5,
    scrollTrigger: {
      trigger: ".ocean-mint",
      start: "top 85%",
      onEnter: () => {
        gsap.to(".ocean-mint", {
          textShadow: "0 0 30px rgba(32, 178, 170, 0.8)",
          duration: 1
        });
      }
    }
  });
}
```

**Deliverables:**
- ✅ Hero section built
- ✅ Text reveals working smoothly
- ✅ Ocean mint glow effects implemented
- ✅ Gradient background animating on scroll

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Text reveals feel cinematic (not rushed)
- Ocean mint glow is subtle, not overwhelming
- Scroll effect feels smooth with Lenis

### Phase 4: Territory Map Particle System (Days 14-21)

**Objectives:**
- Create particle coalescence animation
- Implement zone illumination
- Add magnetic hover effects
- Optimize for mobile performance

**The "WHOA" Moment Implementation:**

**`/src/components/TerritoryMap/MapParticles.js`:**
```javascript
import { gsap, ScrollTrigger } from '../../utils/gsap-config.js';
import { isMobile } from '../../utils/performance-optimizations.js';

class MapParticleSystem {
  constructor(container) {
    this.container = container;
    this.particles = [];
    this.particleCount = isMobile() ? 50 : 200;
    this.init();
  }

  init() {
    // Create particle elements
    for (let i = 0; i < this.particleCount; i++) {
      const particle = document.createElement('div');
      particle.className = 'map-particle';
      particle.style.left = Math.random() * 100 + '%';
      particle.style.top = Math.random() * 100 + '%';
      this.container.appendChild(particle);
      this.particles.push(particle);
    }

    this.animateFormation();
  }

  animateFormation() {
    const tl = gsap.timeline({
      scrollTrigger: {
        trigger: ".map-formation",
        start: "top center",
        end: "center center",
        scrub: 2,
        anticipatePin: 1,
        onUpdate: (self) => {
          // Gradual darken before WHOA moment
          const darkness = Math.min(1, self.progress * 2);
          gsap.set("body", {
            backgroundColor: `rgb(${20 * (1-darkness)}, ${20 * (1-darkness)}, ${20 * (1-darkness)})`
          });
        }
      }
    });

    // Phase 1: Chaos (particles scattered randomly)
    tl.set(this.particles, {
      opacity: 0,
      scale: 0,
      x: (i) => gsap.utils.random(-500, 500),
      y: (i) => gsap.utils.random(-500, 500)
    });

    // Phase 2: Coalesce (particles form Territory Map)
    tl.to(this.particles, {
      opacity: 1,
      scale: 1,
      x: 0,
      y: 0,
      duration: 2,
      stagger: {
        amount: 1.5,
        from: "random"
      },
      ease: "power2.inOut" // Gentle, not aggressive
    });

    // Phase 3: Zone illumination
    tl.from(".zone", {
      opacity: 0,
      scale: 0.8,
      duration: 1,
      stagger: 0.3,
      ease: "back.out(1.7)"
    }, "-=1");

    // Reveal text after map forms
    tl.from(".reveal-after-map", {
      opacity: 0,
      y: 30,
      duration: 1,
      ease: "power3.out"
    });
  }
}

export default MapParticleSystem;
```

**Particle CSS:**
```css
.map-particle {
  position: absolute;
  width: 4px;
  height: 4px;
  background: radial-gradient(circle, var(--ocean-mint-glow) 0%, transparent 70%);
  border-radius: 50%;
  will-change: transform, opacity;
  pointer-events: none;
}

/* Mobile optimization */
@media (max-width: 768px) {
  .map-particle {
    width: 2px;
    height: 2px;
  }
}
```

**Magnetic Zone Hover:**
```javascript
function initZoneHovers() {
  document.querySelectorAll('.zone').forEach(zone => {
    zone.addEventListener('mousemove', (e) => {
      const rect = zone.getBoundingClientRect();
      const x = e.clientX - rect.left - rect.width / 2;
      const y = e.clientY - rect.top - rect.height / 2;

      gsap.to(zone, {
        x: x / 5,
        y: y / 5,
        scale: 1.05,
        duration: 0.5,
        ease: "power2.out"
      });
    });

    zone.addEventListener('mouseleave', () => {
      gsap.to(zone, {
        x: 0,
        y: 0,
        scale: 1,
        duration: 0.5,
        ease: "power2.out"
      });
    });
  });
}
```

**HTML Structure:**
```html
<section class="map-formation" id="territory-map">
  <div class="particle-container">
    <!-- Particles injected by JS -->
  </div>

  <div class="territory-map">
    <div class="zone zone-0" data-zone="0">
      <h3>Zone 0</h3>
      <p>"AI isn't for me"</p>
    </div>

    <div class="zone zone-1" data-zone="1">
      <h3>Zone 1</h3>
      <p>"I could try this"</p>
    </div>

    <!-- Continue for zones 2, 3, 4 -->

    <div class="zone zone-4" data-zone="4">
      <h3>Zone 4</h3>
      <p>"I control the quality"</p>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="map-text reveal-after-map">
    <h2>We don't teach tools. We guide you through territory.</h2>
    <p>In 6 weeks, Zone 0 → early Zone 4</p>
  </div>
</section>
```

### Phase 5: Discord Preview & CTA (Days 22-26)

**Objectives:**

- Implement emoji pulse effects
- Build CTA with hover interactions

**Discord Chat Bubbles:**
```javascript
function initDiscordAnimations() {
  const bubbles = document.querySelectorAll('.chat-bubble');

  gsap.from(bubbles, {
    y: 100,
    opacity: 0,
    rotation: 5,
    duration: 1,
    stagger: {
      amount: 2,
      from: "random"
    },
    ease: "elastic.out(1, 0.5)",
    scrollTrigger: {
      trigger: ".discord-preview",
      start: "top 70%",
      end: "bottom bottom",
      scrub: 1
    }
  });

  // Emoji pulse (continuous)
  gsap.to(".emoji-reaction", {
    scale: 1.2,
    duration: 0.8,
    yoyo: true,
    repeat: -1,
    ease: "power1.inOut",
    stagger: {
      amount: 1,
      from: "random"
    }
  });
}
```

**CTA Button Animation:**
```javascript
function initCTAAnimations() {
  const ctaButton = document.querySelector('.cta-button');

  // Hover effect
  ctaButton.addEventListener('mouseenter', () => {
    gsap.to(ctaButton, {
      scale: 1.05,
      boxShadow: "0 0 30px rgba(32, 178, 170, 0.5)",
      duration: 0.3,
      ease: "power2.out"
    });
  });

  ctaButton.addEventListener('mouseleave', () => {
    gsap.to(ctaButton, {
      scale: 1,
      boxShadow: "0 0 0px rgba(32, 178, 170, 0)",
      duration: 0.3,
      ease: "power2.out"
    });
  });

  // Final scroll reveal
  gsap.from(".cta-section", {
    opacity: 0,
    y: 50,
    duration: 1.5,
    ease: "power3.out",
    scrollTrigger: {
      trigger: ".cta-section",
      start: "top 80%"
    }
  });
}
```

```html

</section>
```

### Phase 6: Performance Optimization (Days 27-29)

**Objectives:**
- Optimize images and assets
- Implement code splitting
- Create mobile-specific optimizations
- Monitor and maintain 60fps

**Image Optimization:**
```javascript
// Convert all images to WebP
// Use responsive srcset
// Implement lazy loading

// Example lazy loading:
const imageObserver = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
  entries.forEach(entry => {
    if (entry.isIntersecting) {
      const img = entry.target;
      img.src = img.dataset.src;
      img.classList.remove('lazy');
      imageObserver.unobserve(img);
    }
  });
});

document.querySelectorAll('img.lazy').forEach(img => {
  imageObserver.observe(img);
});
```

**Code Splitting:**
```javascript
// Dynamic imports for GSAP plugins
const initMapParticles = async () => {
  const { default: MapParticles } = await import('./components/TerritoryMap/MapParticles.js');
  // Initialize...
};
```

**Mobile Optimizations:**
```javascript
ScrollTrigger.matchMedia({
  // Mobile only
  "(max-width: 768px)": function() {
    // Reduce particle count
    // Simplify animations
    // Disable Lenis if performance drops

    // Example: Simplified hero animation
    gsap.from(".hero-title .reveal-text", {
      y: 30,
      opacity: 0,
      stagger: 0.1,
      duration: 0.5,
      ease: "power2.out"
    });
  },

  // Desktop only
  "(min-width: 769px)": function() {
    // Full particle system
    // All animations enabled
  }
});
```

**Performance Budget Targets:**
- First Contentful Paint: < 1.5s
- Time to Interactive: < 3.5s
- Consistent 60fps desktop, 45fps+ mobile
- Lighthouse Score: 90+ Performance

**Deliverables:**
- ✅ All images converted to WebP
- ✅ Lazy loading implemented
- ✅ Code splitting reducing initial bundle
- ✅ Mobile-specific optimizations active
- ✅ Performance targets met

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Lighthouse Performance score 90+
- Real device testing shows 60fps desktop
- Mobile maintains 45fps+ consistently
- No memory leaks (monitor over 10min scroll session)

---

### Phase 7: Testing & Polish (Days 30-32)

**Objectives:**
- Cross-browser testing
- Device testing
- Performance validation
- Accessibility audit
- User acceptance testing

**Testing Protocol:**

**1. Cross-Browser Testing:**
- Chrome (primary development target)
- Safari (iOS + macOS)
- Firefox (secondary)
- Edge (Windows)

**2. Device Testing Matrix:**
| Device Type | Specific Models | Priority |
|-------------|----------------|----------|
| iPhone | 12, 13, 14 Pro | High |
| Android | Samsung Galaxy S21+ | High |
| Tablet | iPad Air, Pro | Medium |
| Desktop | 1920×1080, 2560×1440 | High |

**3. Performance Testing:**
```bash
# Lighthouse CI in CI/CD
npm run lighthouse

# WebPageTest from multiple locations
# - Virginia, USA
# - London, UK
# - Singapore
# - Nairobi, Kenya (your target market)
```

**4. Accessibility Testing:**
- Screen reader compatibility (VoiceOver, TalkBack)
- Keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter, Arrow keys)
- Color contrast ratios (WCAG AA minimum)
- Reduced motion preferences (`prefers-reduced-motion`)

**5. User Acceptance Testing:**
- Recruit 5 users from target demographic
- Ask them to:
  - Scroll through entire page
  - Note moments of confusion or delight
  - Click CTA (even if not real)
- Measure:
  - Scroll depth to CTA (target: 80%+)
  - Time on page (target: 2min+)
  - Qualitative feedback

**Polish Tasks:**
1. Adjust animation timings based on testing
2. Fix any browser-specific bugs
3. Optimize assets further if needed
4. Add loading states (skeleton screens)
5. Create error boundaries

**Deliverables:**
- ✅ All browsers working correctly
- ✅ All devices tested and optimized
- ✅ Performance scores 90+ across board
- ✅ Accessibility audit passed
- ✅ User testing completed with positive feedback

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- No console errors in any browser
- Lighthouse scores: 90+ Performance, 95+ Accessibility
- 4/5 users rate experience as "impressive"
- Scroll depth to CTA > 75%

## 📊 Resource Requirements

### Development Team
| Role | Responsibilities | Time Commitment |
|------|------------------|-----------------|
| Senior Front-End Developer | Lead development, GSAP implementation | 40 hrs/week × 6.5 weeks |
| Motion Design Specialist | Animation polish, particle system | 20 hrs/week × 3 weeks |
| UI/UX Designer | Visual assets, design tokens | 15 hrs/week × 2 weeks |
| QA Engineer | Cross-browser testing, performance | 20 hrs/week × 2 weeks |

### Budget Estimate
| Role | Rate | Hours | Total |
|------|------|-------|-------|
| Senior Front-End Developer | $100-150/hr | 260 hrs | $26,000-39,000 |
| Motion Design Specialist | $80-120/hr | 60 hrs | $4,800-7,200 |
| UI/UX Designer | $75-100/hr | 30 hrs | $2,250-3,000 |
| QA Engineer | $60-80/hr | 40 hrs | $2,400-3,200 |
| **Total** | | | **$35,450-52,400** |

*Alternative: Use agentic developers (Amelia, Barry) to reduce cost significantly*

### Tools & Licenses
| Tool | Cost | Purpose |
|------|------|---------|
| GSAP Club License | $199/year | Commercial use, bonus plugins |
| Figma Professional | $45/month | Design collaboration |
| Lighthouse CI | Free | Performance monitoring |
| WebPageTest | Free | Real-world testing |
| **Total Tools** | **$739/year** | |

---

## 🎯 Success Metrics

### Quantitative Metrics
- **Performance:**
  - First Contentful Paint < 1.5s
  - Time to Interactive < 3.5s
  - Lighthouse Performance Score 90+

- **Engagement:**
  - Scroll depth to CTA > 75%
  - Time on page > 2min
  - Diagnostic form click rate > 15%

- **Technical:**
  - 60fps desktop, 45fps+ mobile
  - Zero console errors
  - < 3s initial page load (3G)

### Qualitative Metrics
- User feedback: "Impressive" or "Beautiful"
- Awwwards submission ready
- Social shares of landing page
- Competitor inquiries about implementation

---

## 🚀 Implementation Timeline

```
Week 1: Phase 0-1 (Foundation + Visual Assets)
Week 2: Phase 2 (Core Animation Infrastructure)
Week 3-4: Phase 3 (Hero Section)
Week 5-7: Phase 4 (Territory Map Particle System) ← CRITICAL
Week 7-8: Phase 5 (Discord Preview + CTA)
Week 8-9: Phase 6 (Performance Optimization)
Week 9-10: Phase 7 (Testing & Polish)
```

**Critical Path:** Territory Map Particle System (Phase 4)
**Risk Mitigation:** Start Phase 4 early, have fallback simple animation ready

---

## 🔄 Risk Mitigation Strategies

### Risk 1: Particle System Performance
**Probability:** Medium
**Impact:** High
**Mitigation:**
- Mobile particle count reduced (50 vs 200)
- CSS-only particles fallback
- Pre-baked animation video option

### Risk 2: Timeline Overrun
**Probability:** Medium
**Impact:** Medium
**Mitigation:**
- Phased rollout (Hero → Map → Discord)
- Simple version of each section ready
- Agentic developers can accelerate

### Risk 3: Cross-Browser Issues
**Probability:** Low
**Impact:** Medium
**Mitigation:**
- Early Safari testing (known GSAP quirks)
- Autoprefixer for CSS
- Fallback animations for older browsers

### Risk 4: Scope Creep
**Probability:** High
**Impact:** Medium
**Mitigation:**
- Strict adherence to this spec
- "Nice-to-have" list for V2
- Daily standups to stay focused

---

## 📝 Handoff Documentation for Agentic Developers

### Getting Started
1. Read this entire document
2. Review Figma design mockups (link TBD)
3. Set up local environment:
   ```bash
   git clone [repo]
   cd k2m-landing
   npm install
   npm run dev
   ```

### Development Workflow
1. Create feature branch: `feature/phase-X-description`
2. Build according to spec
3. Test locally with Lighthouse
4. Submit PR with:
   - Description of changes
   - Lighthouse score screenshots
   - FPS monitor results
   - Browser testing notes

### Code Standards
- Use ES6+ features
- Follow existing naming conventions
- Comment complex animations
- No console.log in production

### Performance Requirements
- All animations must use `transform` and `opacity` (GPU-accelerated)
- `will-change` applied only during active animations
- ScrollTrigger `scrub` value between 1-2 (smooth, not sluggish)
- Mobile first approach (optimize for phones)

### Testing Checklist
- [ ] Chrome (desktop + mobile)
- [ ] Safari (desktop + iOS)
- [ ] Firefox (desktop)
- [ ] Edge (desktop)
- [ ] Lighthouse score 90+
- [ ] FPS monitor shows 60fps desktop
- [ ] Real device testing (iPhone + Android)
- [ ] Accessibility audit passed
- [ ] No console errors
